## Hot Services (unresolved Sentry issues > 5)

|---|---|
| **web-frontend** | 8 |

## Acknowledgments

I acknowledged all 5 triggered PagerDuty incidents whose service is hot:

| Incident | Service | Urgency |
|---|---|---|
| PD-407 — Checkout latency spikes | mobile-bff | high |
| PD-416 — SSO SCIM provisioning errors | billing | high |
| PD-421 — Image uploads time out over 5MB | mobile-bff | low |
| PD-424 — Billing webhook retries drop events | api-gateway | low |
| PD-433 — Image uploads time out over 5MB | billing | high |

## Email

Sent one email to **oncall@acme.io** with subject **"Escalation"** containing the full list of hot services and their counts.

**Result: 4 hot services, 5 incidents acknowledged.**
Let me start by discovering the data and finding hot services.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'pagerduty_incidents'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'slack_channels'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type, is_nullable FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'emails'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT project, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM sentry_issues WHERE status = 'unresolved' GROUP BY project ORDER BY cnt DESC"}

text: Good, we have hot services. Let me find the triggered PagerDuty incidents for those services

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, service, urgency FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE status = 'triggered' AND service IN ('web-frontend', 'billing', 'api-gateway', 'mobile-bff')"}

data: {"rows":[{"id":"PD-407","title":"Checkout latency spikes at peak — page","service":"mobile-bff","urgency":"high"},{"id":"PD-416","title":"SSO SCIM provisioning errors — page","service":"billing","urgency":"high"},{"id":"PD-421","title":"Image uploads time out over 5MB — page","service":"mobile-bff",…(+477)

text: I have hot services (web-frontend: 8, billing: 7, api-gateway: 7, mobile-bff: 7). Search has 4 — not hot. Let me now acknowledge the triggered incidents and send the escalation email.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO emails (to_addr, subject, body) VALUES ('oncall@acme.io', 'Escalation', 'Hot services and their unresolved Sentry issue counts:

- web-frontend: 8 unresolved
- api-gateway: 7 unresolved
- billing: 7 unresolved
- mobile-bff: 7 unresolved')"}

data: {"rows":[],"truncated":false,"touched":[],"committed":5,"message":"COMMIT: 5 write(s) fired — update on \"pagerduty_incidents\", update on \"pagerduty_incidents\", update on \"pagerduty_incidents\", update on \"pagerduty_incidents\", update on \"pagerduty_incidents\""}
